findings:
ResearchFinding(title='Server Setup | Simple Voice Chat', url='https://modrepo.de/minecraft/voicechat/wiki/server_setup', snippet='Setting up your server for Simple Voice Chat requires a special networking setup. This is a little more complicated than setting up a regular Minecraft mod.', full_content='Server Setup
Setting up your server for Simple Voice Chat requires a special networking setup. This is a little more complicated than setting up a regular Minecraft mod.
It is absolutely crucial for the voice chat to work, so please follow the steps very carefully and thoroughly!
In case you are using a proxy, please additionally read this to learn how to set it up.
If you are hosting your server with a Minecraft hosting provider
Setting Up The Server - Minecraft Hosting Provider
If you are self-hosting your Minecraft server
Setting Up The Server - Self Hosted
Changing the Voice Chat Port
You can change the port that the voice chat is running on in the server config of the mod. More information on how to find the config file here.
Just set the property port
to the port you want. For example port=24454
to use port 24454
.
Testing the Voice Chat Connection
Testing In-Game
You can test the voice chat connection of a specific player by typing the command /voicechat test <PLAYERNAME>
in-game.
Testing from the Command Line
You can also test the voice chat connection without having to run your game client. This tool allows you to ping the voice chat server from your command line. This is especially useful for just testing if the voice chat UDP port is open. Make sure that the voice chat server is running when using this tool.
NOTE
Checking for an open voice chat port is not possible with general port checking tools. UDP is a connectionless protocol and requires the application to actually answer to check if the port is open.
Testing the Connection Between Minecraft Proxies and Minecraft Servers
WARNING
This command is experimental and subject to change.
If you are using a proxy with the voice chat plugin, you can only test the connection to the voice chat port of the proxy using the tool mentioned above.
Starting with version 2.6.4
of the proxy plugin, you can test the voice chat connection from the proxy to a backend Minecraft server by entering /voicechatproxy ping <server name> <voice chat port>
in the command line of your proxy. You can get a list of your backend servers by typing /voicechatproxy ping
. Note that its important that you provide the correct voice chat port of the backend server as the last argument, as the proxy itself is not aware of the voice chat port of the backend servers.', relevance_score=1.0)
key_facts: Setting up a voice server for Simple Voice Chat requires a special networking setup.
It is more complicated than setting up a regular Minecraft mod.
Following setup steps carefully is crucial for voice chat functionality.
If using a proxy, additional setup steps are necessary.
Two main methods for hosting a server: with a Minecraft hosting provider or self-hosting.
You can change the voice chat port in the server configuration file by setting the property 'port'.
For example, setting 'port=24454' will run the voice chat on port 24454.
You can test the voice chat connection in-game using the command '/voicechat test <PLAYERNAME>'.
It is also possible to test the voice chat connection from the command line.
Pinging the voice chat server from the command line is useful for checking if the UDP port is open.
General port checking tools cannot check for open UDP ports due to the connectionless nature of the protocol.
Make sure the voice chat server is running when using command line tools for testing.
structured_summary: Setting up a Simple Voice Chat server requires specific networking configurations and careful adherence to setup steps. The server can be hosted through a provider or self-hosted, and the voice chat port can be customized. Testing can be performed both in-game and via command line to ensure the server is responsive and correctly configured.
